ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Wunba sikint u mɔŋ ki u binbeŋ tee kaman nan bik na binbeŋ na, ŋɔɔe tan sii tee saakɔɔ Yennu naan ni.
translated: The old man said that if someone steals something and hides it, he will be caught and brought to Yennu.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yisa nba daa be piak linba na ki siab nyii saakɔɔ na ŋaak ni ki baar ki tan yetɔ a “A bonpobik na taan kpowa. Bee ki a ji mukis Wanntɔɔ na?”
translated: He said, "I will go and see if there is any food left. If there is, I will bring some for you."

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Li-i tee ki i nyikit i leeb biit ki chabitibi, i Baa Yennu nba be sanpaapo na mun sa nyik ki chabiiwa;
translated: The one who does not want to work will not eat, says Baa Yennu, the one who does not want to work will not get anything.

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Ŋanne ki u poorpoweiteeb baar u boor ni ki tan boiɔ a “A mi nan Farisiinba na nba gbat a maan maŋ na, bi wutoa doo a paakawa-aa?”
translated: One day, as they were walking along the road, they saw a man sitting by the roadside and they asked him, “Sir, are you a Pharisee?”

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: ŋaan betib a “Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ŋɔɔ Yennu yet a ‘N ŋaak na sii tee Yenmiaru ŋaake,’ ŋaan yimme kpintir ki teen fatiteeb kinkar boor.”
translated: He said, "When Yennu was young, he said, 'I will not be called Yenmiaru,' and he went to live in a different place."

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: U din be leŋe nan yoo nba ki Bat Herod din tan kpo na. Linba kur din tun a lin gbeen linba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu din te ki u sɔkiniisɔɔ pak li po nee a “N din nyinn n bija maŋ Ijipt tingbouŋ nie.” Bonjabis kpinu labaar
translated: They said that the people who were there were not able to understand the language of Herod. So they spoke to him in the language of the Yomdaanɔ Yennu, and he said to them, “I do not know the language of Egypt.”

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Gotir nɔɔnii nba yuuk ki lin na; bi ki buuri; bi bia ki jaan ki wuu suka nan naapaat ni, ŋaan ki i Baa Yennu nba be sanpaapo na dintib. I ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki gar nɔɔnii-ii?
translated: Gotir asked his father for some food; he said he was hungry; he said he wanted to eat some meat with some pounded yam, and that he would go to Baa Yennu's place to get some firewood. What did his father say to him?

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N beerie na, barbunu daar, Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan ba din piak baryana kur nba na paak,
translated: “I went to the farm, I saw a woman, she was weeding the millet, she was very tired, I helped her.”

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ŋaan bant man nan li-i tee ki ŋaadaanɔ poŋ mi yoo nba ki nanyukɔɔ yaa wun baar u ŋaak ni nyiɔku, u sii kare ki guu; u kan sak ki nanyukɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ ki jamɔ.
translated: The old man said that the young man should go and learn a trade so that he can become rich and marry a wife and build a house.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba kpaau ki tabinɔ dapunpunn paak gbenn yoonba, ki bi tɔ tiatia ki chent chent u tiat na,
translated: The child who is playing with the mud, he will grow up to become a strong man, and he will build a big house.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ki leer mun din baa tanpuut paak, siaminba ki tant ki yabi, ki li din pia ki kpaat yiama, kimaan tant ki yab leŋi,
translated: If you see a person who is not your friend, do not greet them, but if you see a friend, greet them well, and if you see an elder, greet them respectfully.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Yimm jɔɔndat teeb na, li sii bi i paak. I want niib na a ‘Sɔɔ-i por Yennu diiuk, li kaa biiti, a ŋaan li-i tee ki sɔɔ por likirii nba be diiuk maŋ ni na, a see ki u gbeen wa por biaŋinba na.’
translated: “The old man said, ‘Listen, my children. I want you to know that ‘Sɔɔ-i por Yennu diiuk, li kaa biiti, a ŋaan li-i tee ki sɔɔ por likirii nba be diiuk maŋ ni na, a see ki u gbeen wa por biaŋinba na.’”

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Ki subindamm na jiin a ‘Aaii, ti yara mun kan jaŋ timm nan yimmi. I saa man binba kɔi kpan boor ni, ki saa daa i mɔŋ yara.’
translated: The old woman said, "My child, I am telling you the truth. If you don't listen to my advice, you will regret it."

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: ki bi tiin saa tingbouŋ maŋ loka kur po ki jii bi baatib ki baat u boor ni. Ba tuu gbat ki u be siaminba kur po, bi din tuu jii bi baatib lianii paake ki saan namm u boor ni.
translated: The woman went to the farm to weed. She worked hard and finished weeding the farm. When she was done, she went home to cook food for her children.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Li tee kaman nan kanpotik binn nba ki sɔɔ jii ki bur kpaab ni, ki li waar ki gar tingbouŋ na bonboorii kura,
translated: The person who does not know how to greet others properly will not be able to get help from anyone, because they will be seen as someone who does not respect others.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ki Yisa betɔ a “N sa saan ki saa te wun paak.”
translated: He said to them, "I will not go until you all come."

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Ki Yisa lek ki fiin ba yet linba na, ŋaan bet saakɔɔ na a “Daa tiin jaŋmaanii. Teentin yada kuukɔɔ.”
translated: When Yisa saw that he was about to die, he said to his son, "Take care of my wife. Bury me properly."

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki bonpobik na fiir set kakibii, ki somm lin. Ki li yaar ŋaan litib bonchiann.
translated: The woman who is going to the farm is carrying a basket of shea nuts, she is going to sell them. She is going to buy some food.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ki leer mun din baa tanpuut paak, siaminba ki tant ki yabi, ki li din pia ki kpaat yiama, kimaan tant ki yab leŋi,
translated: If you see a person who is doing something wrong, tell him to stop, because it is not good to do bad things, and if he refuses to listen, leave him alone.

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yisa nba din nyii Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki saa na, ki u poorpoweiteeb din baar u boor ni ki tan wantɔ jiantu ŋaak maŋ diit.
translated: Yisa told Yennu that he should not be afraid, because he will help him, and he will give him a good wife.
